seoTitle: How to Record a Partial Invoice Payment in QBO
metaDescription: Learn how to record a partial payment for an invoice in QuickBooks Online so the remaining balance stays accurate until the customer pays in full.
intro: Not every customer pays their invoice in one go, and QuickBooks Online makes it possible to log a partial payment while keeping the remaining balance visible. This guide covers how to enter a partial payment correctly so nothing gets lost in your accounts receivable.

steps.heading: Open the invoice
steps.body: Go to the specific invoice that's receiving a partial payment.

steps.heading: Select receive payment
steps.body: Use the receive payment option tied to that invoice rather than creating a new transaction from scratch.

steps.heading: Enter the partial amount
steps.body: Type in the amount actually received instead of the full invoice total, leaving the rest as an open balance.

steps.heading: Save and confirm the remaining balance
steps.body: Save the payment and check that the invoice now shows the correct outstanding amount.

faq.question: Will the invoice still show as open after a partial payment?
faq.answer: Yes, the invoice stays open and shows the remaining balance until it's paid in full.

faq.question: Can I apply multiple partial payments to one invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, you can record as many partial payments as needed until the invoice balance reaches zero.

faq.question: Does QuickBooks send a receipt for partial payments?
faq.answer: Depending on your settings, QuickBooks can send a payment confirmation showing the amount received and remaining balance.

faq.question: What if I accidentally enter the wrong partial amount?
faq.answer: You can edit the payment transaction afterward to correct the amount if it was entered incorrectly.
